prerequisites: A Finnworlds account, created at finnworlds.com by submitting your name and email.
An active Finnworlds plan, which provides the API key and determines your monthly call quota and per-minute rate limit.
A start date (start_date) marking the earliest point from which to retrieve data.
Optionally, the specific tickers, countries, commodities, bond_type, list, or list_countries_for_bonds values you want to scope the synced streams to.

authMethods.name: API Key
authMethods.summary: Finnworlds authenticates every request with a single API key, supplied as the connector secret field `key`. Finnworlds expects it as the `key` query parameter on each API call (for example, ?key=YOUR-KEY against https://api.finnworlds.com/api/v1/). There is no OAuth or username/password flow.

setupSteps.title: Create a Finnworlds account and choose a plan
setupSteps.body: Go to finnworlds.com and sign up with your name and email. Select a plan from the pricing page; access to the financial data APIs requires a paid plan, which sets your monthly call quota and per-minute rate limit.

setupSteps.title: Retrieve your API key
setupSteps.body: After registering and choosing a plan, obtain your API key from Finnworlds. This key is passed to the API as the `key` query parameter and is the only credential the connector needs.

setupSteps.title: Enter the API key and start date
setupSteps.body: In the connector configuration, paste your Finnworlds API key into the `key` field (the required secret) and set the required `start_date` to the earliest date you want data pulled from.

setupSteps.title: Scope the optional filters
setupSteps.body: Optionally narrow the synced data using the connector's optional fields: `tickers` (stock symbols such as AAPL or GOOG), `countries`, `commodities`, `bond_type` (durations such as 10y, 5y, 2y), `list`, and `list_countries_for_bonds`. Leave any of these blank to use the connector defaults.
